channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: AWE Tech Innovation Challenge 2026: Apply for Up to US$300,000 in Equity Investment for Impact Startups in India
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Technology-driven startups tackling some of India’s most pressing development challenges now have an exciting opportunity to secure patient equity capital through the AWE Tech Innovation Challenge 2026. Managed by AWE Funds, powered by the Evergreen Philanthropic Fund (EPF), and supported by the Bayer Foundation and AWE Foundation, the challenge is designed to identify and invest […]

The post AWE Tech Innovation Challenge 2026: Apply for Up to US$300,000 in Equity Investment for Impact Startups in India appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Technology-driven startups tackling some of India’s most pressing development challenges now have an exciting opportunity to secure patient equity capital through the AWE Tech Innovation Challenge 2026.

Managed by AWE Funds, powered by the Evergreen Philanthropic Fund (EPF), and supported by the Bayer Foundation and AWE Foundation, the challenge is designed to identify and invest in innovative startups creating measurable impact in healthcare, climate resilience, sustainable agriculture, and women’s empowerment.

Selected companies will receive equity investments ranging from US$100,000 to US$300,000, alongside strategic support, investor access, and valuable industry connections to help accelerate their growth.

About the AWE Tech Innovation Challenge

The AWE Tech Innovation Challenge seeks to support entrepreneurs building scalable technology solutions that improve the lives of underserved and climate-vulnerable communities across India.

Unlike traditional grant programmes, the initiative provides long-term patient equity capital, enabling startups to grow sustainably without the pressure of rapid exits.

The programme focuses on companies capable of delivering both commercial success and measurable social impact.

Funding Opportunity

Successful startups may receive:

  • Equity investment between US$100,000 and US$300,000
  • Funding for up to five startups
  • Long-term patient capital from an evergreen investment fund
  • Access to investors and industry experts
  • Strategic business support
  • Corporate introductions
  • Networking opportunities
  • Direct interaction with the AWE investment team

Funding decisions are made solely at the discretion of the AWE Funds investment team.

Who Should Apply?

The challenge welcomes startups that are:

Early-Stage Startups

Innovative companies with validated technology solutions that are beginning to scale.

Growth-Stage Startups

Businesses with demonstrated traction seeking investment to accelerate expansion.

Technology-Led Ventures

Companies leveraging technology as the core driver of their solution.

Deep-Tech Startups

Ventures applying advanced scientific or engineering innovations to solve complex challenges.

Impact Enterprises

Businesses committed to creating measurable improvements in people’s lives while building sustainable commercial models.

Priority Sectors

The programme focuses on four major sectors that address India’s most urgent development needs.

Healthcare

Technology solutions improving healthcare access, affordability, quality, diagnostics, treatment, and health outcomes.

Sustainable Agriculture

Innovations supporting farmers through:

  • Smart farming
  • Precision agriculture
  • Digital agriculture
  • Supply chain improvements
  • Agricultural productivity
  • Climate-smart farming

Women’s Empowerment

Solutions that strengthen women’s:

  • Economic participation
  • Financial inclusion
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Health
  • Education
  • Agency and decision-making

Climate

Technology innovations addressing:

  • Climate resilience
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Resource efficiency
  • Adaptation
  • Climate-smart development

Impact Areas

The initiative aims to contribute toward ambitious impact targets, including:

  • Improving health outcomes for 1 million people
  • Supporting 1.5 million women to gain greater agency
  • Improving livelihoods for 500,000 rural and small-town residents, including farmers

Applicants should clearly demonstrate how their solution contributes to measurable social and environmental outcomes.

What Selected Startups Receive

Beyond financial investment, selected companies gain access to a comprehensive support ecosystem.

Patient Equity Capital

Unlike many investment programmes focused on rapid exits, AWE Funds provides long-term investment designed to help companies scale sustainably.

Industry Connections

Founders receive warm introductions to:

  • Potential customers
  • Corporate partners
  • Strategic collaborators
  • Ecosystem partners

Direct Investor Access

Every application is reviewed directly by the AWE investment team without intermediaries, ensuring founders receive genuine consideration.

Progressive Investment Journey

High-performing startups advance through multiple evaluation stages, gaining increasing exposure to experienced investors and industry leaders.

Selection Process

Applications progress through several competitive stages.

Stage 1: Application Review

The investment team evaluates and scores all submitted applications.

Stage 2: Top 20

The twenty highest-scoring startups present to the AWE Leadership Team.

Stage 3: Top 10

Selected startups pitch to industry experts.

Stage 4: Top 5

The final five startups advance directly to the Investment Committee for funding consideration.

This staged process provides multiple opportunities for feedback, exposure, and investor engagement.

Information Required During Application

Applicants should be prepared to provide detailed business information, including:

  • Startup name
  • Website
  • LinkedIn profile
  • Business location
  • Sector
  • Problem statement
  • Solution description
  • Social impact achieved
  • Founder information
  • Educational background
  • Professional experience
  • Revenue model
  • Annual revenue
  • Current fundraising details
  • Existing investor commitments
  • Company valuation
  • Previous funding history
  • Market size (TAM/SAM)
  • Obtainable market size
  • Industry growth rate
  • Competitor analysis
  • Pitch deck (PDF)

Preparing this information in advance can help ensure a smoother application process.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 31 July 2026

Interested founders are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues.

Apply Here 

Why Startups Should Apply

Securing capital remains one of the biggest challenges for impact startups, particularly those operating in underserved markets.

The AWE Tech Innovation Challenge provides much more than funding—it offers founders the opportunity to connect with experienced investors, industry experts, strategic partners, and a long-term investment platform committed to scaling impact.

For startups building innovative technology solutions in healthcare, agriculture, women’s empowerment, or climate resilience, this challenge offers a valuable opportunity to accelerate growth while expanding positive social impact across India.

For more information on this grant visit here 

Discover more grant opportunities on OFY here 

The post AWE Tech Innovation Challenge 2026: Apply for Up to US$300,000 in Equity Investment for Impact Startups in India appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AWIEF Pitch n Grow 2026: Applications Open for Africa’s Leading Women-Led Deep Technology Startup Competition
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
African women entrepreneurs are driving some of the continent’s most transformative innovations, and the African Women Innovation and Entrepreneurship Forum (AWIEF) is once again creating a platform to identify, nurture, and elevate these innovators through its flagship Pitch n Grow 2026 Competition. Applications are now open for the AWIEF Pitch n Grow 2026 programme, a […]

The post AWIEF Pitch n Grow 2026: Applications Open for Africa’s Leading Women-Led Deep Technology Startup Competition appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
African women entrepreneurs are driving some of the continent’s most transformative innovations, and the African Women Innovation and Entrepreneurship Forum (AWIEF) is once again creating a platform to identify, nurture, and elevate these innovators through its flagship Pitch n Grow 2026 Competition.

Applications are now open for the AWIEF Pitch n Grow 2026 programme, a continent-wide entrepreneurship competition designed to discover and accelerate promising women-led ventures across Africa. The initiative provides entrepreneurs with mentorship, business development training, visibility, networking opportunities, investor exposure, and the chance to pitch on a global stage.

Under the theme “Deep Roots. Digital Futures.”, the 2026 edition seeks to spotlight women founders leveraging deep technology to address Africa’s most pressing challenges while building scalable and globally relevant solutions.

About AWIEF Pitch n Grow 2026

The AWIEF Pitch n Grow Competition is the organization’s flagship entrepreneurship development programme focused on supporting women entrepreneurs at various stages of business growth.

The programme goes beyond a traditional pitch competition by providing structured support that helps entrepreneurs strengthen their ventures, refine their business models, improve their investment readiness, and increase their visibility across the African entrepreneurship ecosystem.

Participants gain access to:

  • Business development support
  • Entrepreneurial training
  • Expert mentorship
  • Pitch coaching
  • Networking opportunities
  • Media exposure
  • Investor visibility
  • Travel support for finalists
  • Cash prizes for winners

The programme aims to help women founders scale their impact while contributing to economic growth, innovation, and job creation across Africa.

Theme for 2026: Deep Roots. Digital Futures.

Every edition of Pitch n Grow aligns with AWIEF’s broader vision for Africa’s economic transformation.

For 2026, the competition focuses on Deep Technology, emphasizing solutions built from a profound understanding of African realities and challenges.

Rather than simply importing technological solutions, AWIEF is encouraging founders to build technologies that are:

  • Locally rooted
  • Community-informed
  • African-led
  • Globally competitive
  • Scalable
  • Sustainable

The theme recognizes that Africa’s greatest advantage lies not only in technological adoption but also in the deep local knowledge, experiences, and insights that entrepreneurs bring to solving complex problems.

AWIEF is seeking ventures that combine cutting-edge technology with authentic African problem-solving approaches.

Competition Tracks

To ensure fair competition and appropriate support, applicants are grouped into three categories based on business maturity.

1. Idea Track

Designed for entrepreneurs at the earliest stage of development.

Requirements include:

  • No revenue requirement
  • Prototype or proof of concept available
  • Less than 18 months of operation
  • Solo founder or small team

This track supports innovators who are still validating their concepts and preparing for market entry.

2. Startup Track

Created for businesses that have launched and begun attracting customers.

Eligible businesses must:

  • Generate revenue
  • Be formally registered
  • Have operated between 6 months and 3 years
  • Have an active founding team

This category focuses on helping startups strengthen their foundations and prepare for growth.

3. Scale-Up Track

Designed for established ventures experiencing growth and expansion.

Businesses must demonstrate:

  • Revenue above US$500,000 or institutional funding
  • Expansion into multiple African countries
  • More than three years of operation
  • A growing workforce

The scale-up category supports businesses seeking regional or continental growth.

Priority Innovation Areas

AWIEF is particularly interested in ventures applying advanced technologies to solve major development challenges.

Priority sectors include:

Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning

  • Predictive analytics
  • AI-powered decision-making tools
  • Localized AI applications

AgriTech

  • Smart farming technologies
  • Precision agriculture
  • Crop monitoring systems
  • Agricultural data platforms

HealthTech

  • Digital healthcare
  • Telemedicine
  • AI diagnostics
  • Medical innovations

Clean Energy

  • Smart energy systems
  • Renewable energy platforms
  • Grid optimization technologies

Water Technology

  • Water management solutions
  • Climate adaptation technologies
  • Smart irrigation systems

Biotechnology

  • Agricultural biotechnology
  • Synthetic biology
  • Pharmaceutical innovation

FinTech

  • Digital financial services
  • Blockchain applications
  • Financial inclusion technologies

Cybersecurity

  • Digital identity solutions
  • Data protection tools
  • Fraud prevention technologies

EdTech

  • Personalized learning systems
  • AI-enabled education platforms
  • Skills development technologies

Smart Cities

  • Urban technology solutions
  • Intelligent transportation systems
  • Digital infrastructure innovations

Circular Economy Solutions

  • Waste management technologies
  • Sustainable packaging
  • Recycling innovations

Frontier Technologies

  • Satellite applications
  • Autonomous systems
  • Emerging deep-tech solutions

Benefits of Participating

The AWIEF Pitch n Grow programme offers significantly more than prize money.

Selected participants can benefit from:

Business Training

Participants receive structured entrepreneurial training designed to strengthen business models and improve growth strategies.

Expert Mentorship

Founders gain access to experienced entrepreneurs, investors, and industry experts who provide guidance throughout the programme.

International Visibility

The competition offers significant exposure through AWIEF’s extensive network across Africa and beyond.

Global Pitch Platform

Finalists will pitch their businesses before a high-profile audience at the AWIEF 2026 Conference.

Travel Support

Selected finalists receive support to attend the live finals.

Media and Public Relations Exposure

Participants gain opportunities to showcase their ventures to broader audiences.

Cash Awards

Winning ventures will receive cash prizes to support business growth and expansion.

Competition Timeline

Phase 1: Applications Open

22 June 2026

Phase 2: Orientation Webinars

1 – 7 July 2026

Phase 3: Application Screening

Applications Close: 20 July 2026

The screening process will narrow applicants from approximately 200 ventures to 70 teams.

Phase 4: Training Bootcamp

1 – 28 August 2026

Participants will engage in six structured training sessions.

Phase 5: First Submission and Virtual Judging

1 – 15 September 2026

Phase 6: Finalists Announcement

By 20 September 2026

Twelve finalists will be selected.

Phase 7: Mentorship and Pitch Preparation

1 – 31 October 2026

Phase 8: Live Finals

10 – 11 November 2026

The final competition will take place during the AWIEF 2026 Conference.

Application Deadline

Deadline: 20 July 2026

Entrepreneurs are encouraged to submit applications early and participate in the orientation webinars to better understand the programme requirements and evaluation criteria.

Apply Here 

Why Women Entrepreneurs Should Apply

Women continue to face challenges in accessing capital, networks, and growth opportunities. AWIEF Pitch n Grow directly addresses these barriers by creating an ecosystem that supports women-led innovation and entrepreneurship.

Whether you are developing an AI platform, a climate-tech solution, a biotechnology innovation, a fintech product, or another deep-tech venture designed for African markets, Pitch n Grow 2026 offers a unique opportunity to accelerate your business and expand your impact.

The programme is particularly valuable for founders seeking visibility, strategic partnerships, investment readiness, and continental recognition.

With Africa’s innovation ecosystem growing rapidly, AWIEF Pitch n Grow 2026 provides an ideal platform for ambitious women entrepreneurs to showcase their solutions and contribute to shaping Africa’s digital future.

For more information on this opportunity visit here

Discover more opportunities on OFY, click here 

The post AWIEF Pitch n Grow 2026: Applications Open for Africa’s Leading Women-Led Deep Technology Startup Competition appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of Ghana Graduate Admissions Guide for Ghanaian and International Applicants: Complete Application Process, Requirements, and Important Documents
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The University of Ghana has streamlined its graduate admissions process to make it easier for prospective students from Ghana and around the world to apply for Master’s and Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) programmes. Whether you are a Ghanaian applicant, an international student, or a Ghanaian living abroad, understanding the application requirements and procedures is essential […]

The post University of Ghana Graduate Admissions Guide for Ghanaian and International Applicants: Complete Application Process, Requirements, and Important Documents appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Ghana has streamlined its graduate admissions process to make it easier for prospective students from Ghana and around the world to apply for Master’s and Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) programmes. Whether you are a Ghanaian applicant, an international student, or a Ghanaian living abroad, understanding the application requirements and procedures is essential for a successful admission process.

This comprehensive guide explains the University of Ghana graduate application process, required documents, eligibility requirements, and important information applicants must know before submitting their applications.

About the University of Ghana Graduate Admissions Process

The University of Ghana welcomes applications from qualified candidates seeking admission into its various graduate programmes. The institution has established a structured online application system that enables applicants to submit their credentials and supporting documents electronically.

Prospective students are encouraged to carefully review all application requirements and ensure that every necessary document is uploaded before submission. Failure to provide the required documents may result in delays or the inability to process an application.

Who Can Apply?

The University of Ghana graduate admissions portal is open to:

  • Ghanaian applicants
  • International applicants
  • Ghanaians residing abroad
  • Applicants seeking Master’s degree programmes
  • Applicants seeking Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) programmes

Graduate Programmes Available

Applicants may apply for graduate studies across various academic disciplines offered by the University of Ghana through its colleges, including:

  1. College of Basic and Applied Sciences
  2. College of Education
  3. College of Health Sciences
  4. College of Humanities

Candidates should review the specific programme requirements for their chosen field before submitting an application.

Required Documents for Master’s Degree Applicants

Applicants seeking admission into Master’s degree programmes who did not graduate from the University of Ghana must upload the following documents:

Mandatory Documents

  • Bachelor’s Degree Certificate
  • Official Bachelor’s Degree Transcript

These documents must be scanned and attached to the online application portal before submission.

Required Documents for Doctor of Philosophy Applicants

Applicants applying for Doctor of Philosophy programmes must provide more extensive documentation.

Mandatory Documents

  • Bachelor’s Degree Certificate
  • Master’s Degree Certificate
  • Official Bachelor’s Degree Transcript
  • Official Master’s Degree Transcript

Research Proposal Requirement

All Doctor of Philosophy applicants must submit a research proposal.

The proposal must:

  • Be attached to the online application
  • Not exceed four pages
  • Clearly outline the proposed area of research
  • Demonstrate the applicant’s research interests and academic preparedness

Referee Requirements for All Applicants

Every applicant must provide details of three referees during the application process.

Important Points

  • Three referee reports are mandatory
  • Referee information must be submitted online
  • Applicants should ensure referees are informed in advance
  • Referees should be able to provide academic or professional assessments of the applicant

Failure to provide referee details may affect the processing of an application.

Transcript Upload Guidelines

Applicants are required to prepare their academic transcripts according to the University’s specifications.

Transcript Submission Instructions

  • Scan all transcript pages clearly
  • Combine transcript pages into a single PDF document or one Word document
  • Upload the completed file through the online application system
  • Ensure all information is legible and complete

Submitting incomplete or unclear transcripts may delay the admissions review process.

Special Requirement for University of Ghana Graduates

Applicants who graduated from the University of Ghana between 1996 and the present date must provide their Student Identity Number.

Why This Is Important

The Student Identity Number helps the University verify academic records efficiently and facilitates the processing of applications.

Applicants should ensure this information is entered correctly before final submission.

Name Change Documentation Requirement

Applicants whose current names differ from those appearing on their certificates or transcripts must provide supporting documentation.

Required Document

  • Gazette publication showing the official name change

This document must be scanned and uploaded together with the application.

Important Notice Regarding Supporting Documents

The University of Ghana has emphasized that applications will not be processed without the necessary supporting documents.

Applications Will Not Be Processed Without:

  • Official certificates
  • Official transcripts
  • Gazette publication where a name change has occurred

Applicants should verify that all required documents have been uploaded successfully before completing the application process.

Original Documents Verification

Although applications are submitted online, applicants should keep their original documents available.

The University may request original copies during interviews or other verification stages of the admissions process.

Step-by-Step Application Process

Step 1: Review Programme Requirements

Carefully review the admission requirements for your intended graduate programme.

Step 2: Prepare Supporting Documents

Gather and scan all required documents, including:

  • Academic certificates
  • Official transcripts
  • Research proposal (for PhD applicants)
  • Gazette publication (where applicable)

Step 3: Secure Referees

Identify and notify three referees who will support your application.

Step 4: Complete the Online Application

Access the University of Ghana application portal and complete all required sections accurately.

Step 5: Upload Documents

Attach all required documents in the specified format.

Step 6: Verify Information

Review all entered information carefully before submission.

Step 7: Submit Application

Submit your completed application through the official University of Ghana online portal.

Why Careful Application Preparation Matters

A complete and accurate application improves the efficiency of the admissions review process. Missing documents, incomplete information, or incorrect uploads may result in delays or prevent an application from being processed.

Applicants are therefore encouraged to:

  • Read all instructions carefully
  • Upload clear and complete documents
  • Ensure referee details are accurate
  • Submit all required materials before completing the application

Application Deadline

No specific application deadline was stated in the information provided. Applicants are advised to visit the official University of Ghana Graduate Admissions Portal regularly for updates on admission timelines and programme-specific deadlines.

Official Application Portal

Prospective students should complete their applications through the official University of Ghana Graduate Admissions Portal.

Applying through the official portal ensures secure submission of documents and proper processing of applications.

GHANAIANS APPLICANTS

INTERNATIONAL APPLICANTS

Final Thoughts

The University of Ghana offers a well-structured graduate admissions process for local and international applicants seeking advanced academic qualifications. By preparing all required documents, securing referee reports, and following the application instructions carefully, applicants can improve their chances of a smooth and successful admissions experience.

Prospective Master’s and Doctor of Philosophy candidates should begin preparing their documents early and ensure all requirements are fulfilled before submitting their applications through the University’s online portal.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover other open admissions on OFY here.

The post University of Ghana Graduate Admissions Guide for Ghanaian and International Applicants: Complete Application Process, Requirements, and Important Documents appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Undergraduate Scholarship Covering 100% Tuition, Living Expenses, Accommodation Support, and More
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Applications Now Open for the RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship 2026 Students from disadvantaged backgrounds in Vietnam now have an exceptional opportunity to pursue a world-class university education through the RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship 2026. Designed to support talented students who would otherwise face barriers to accessing higher education, this prestigious scholarship provides comprehensive financial assistance […]

The post RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Undergraduate Scholarship Covering 100% Tuition, Living Expenses, Accommodation Support, and More appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications Now Open for the RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship 2026

Students from disadvantaged backgrounds in Vietnam now have an exceptional opportunity to pursue a world-class university education through the RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship 2026. Designed to support talented students who would otherwise face barriers to accessing higher education, this prestigious scholarship provides comprehensive financial assistance throughout the recipient’s entire undergraduate program.

The scholarship goes beyond covering tuition fees. Successful applicants will receive support for educational expenses, living costs, accommodation, travel, and essential study equipment, ensuring that financial challenges do not prevent deserving students from achieving their academic and career goals.

With only two scholarships available, the program is highly competitive and seeks applicants who demonstrate strong academic potential, leadership qualities, resilience, and a commitment to making a positive impact in their communities.

Scholarship Overview

Scholarship Provider

RMIT Vietnam

Scholarship Name

RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship 2026

Application Opening Date

12 January 2026

Application Deadline

3 July 2026, 5:00 PM GMT+7

Number of Scholarships Available

2

Scholarship Duration

Entire undergraduate program

What Does the RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship Cover?

The RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship is a fully funded scholarship package designed to remove financial barriers to higher education.

Recipients will receive:

1. Full Tuition Fee Coverage

  • 100% coverage of English language tuition fees (if applicable).
  • 100% coverage of higher education tuition fees for the entire duration of the program.

2. Compulsory University Fees

  • Coverage of RMIT Vietnam’s compulsory non-academic fees.

3. Monthly Living Stipend

  • VND 6,000,000 per month to support living expenses.

4. Accommodation Support

  • VND 5,000,000 per month accommodation allowance (where applicable).

5. Home Travel Support

  • VND 1,000,000 travel allowance each semester (where applicable).

6. Laptop Support

  • A one-time provision of a laptop to assist with academic studies.

These benefits ensure that scholars can focus fully on their education without worrying about major financial constraints.

Who Is Eligible for the RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship?

To be considered for the scholarship, applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements:

Citizenship Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Be citizens of Vietnam.

Academic Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Have completed high school.
  • Have achieved a minimum Grade 12 GPA of 7.0 out of 10.0.

Financial and Social Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Be financially disadvantaged and/or
  • Have a disability.

Examples of eligible disabilities may include:

  • Long-term physical impairments.
  • Long-term mental health conditions.
  • Cognitive impairments.
  • Sensory impairments.
  • Long-term medical conditions.

Community Organization Referral Requirement

Applicants must be referred by a reputable community organization.

Examples include:

  • REACH
  • KOTO
  • Vietnam Blind Association
  • Sao Mai Center for the Blind
  • Loretto
  • Christina Noble Children’s Foundation
  • HeartReach
  • Smile
  • DRD
  • Local Department of Labour, War Invalids and Social Affairs

Other reputable organizations may also be considered.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be assessed holistically, taking into account applicants’ educational opportunities, personal circumstances, health conditions, and family background.

Academic Potential

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong academic performance.
  • Evidence of achievement in school or educational activities.
  • Ability to submit a clear, compelling, and well-written application.

Professional and Leadership Potential

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Self-awareness and personal development.
  • Clear academic and career aspirations.
  • Realistic and achievable life goals.
  • Leadership experience.
  • Initiative and teamwork skills.
  • Participation in school or community activities.

Personal Attributes

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Positive personal values.
  • Active involvement in community initiatives.
  • Commitment to helping others.
  • Personal resilience and determination.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must prepare the following documents before submitting their application.

Academic Documents

  • Notarized copy of High School Diploma.
  • Academic transcripts.

Students graduating in 2026 may submit their diploma later, but it must be provided no later than 18 August 2026.

Personal Identification Documents

  • One passport-style photograph (3×4 size) taken within six months of application.
  • Copy of national ID card or passport.

Additional Supporting Documents

  • Current Curriculum Vitae (CV).
  • Evidence of English language proficiency (if available).
  • Evidence of financial circumstances.
  • Evidence of disability or health condition (if applicable).

Personal Statement Requirements

Applicants must submit a personal statement between 300 and 500 words.

The statement should address:

  1. Intended undergraduate program and academic goals.
  2. Career aspirations and future plans.
  3. How the chosen program will help achieve those goals.
  4. Why the applicant deserves the scholarship.
  5. Academic achievements.
  6. Extracurricular activities.
  7. Community involvement.
  8. Leadership experiences.

A strong personal statement should clearly demonstrate both need and potential.

Reference Letter Requirements

Applicants must submit two reference letters.

Each letter should contain between 300 and 500 words.

Reference Letter One

Must come from a community organization where the applicant has been involved for at least one year through activities such as:

  • Volunteering.
  • Employment.
  • Sponsorship programs.
  • Community engagement initiatives.

Reference Letter Two

Must come from:

  • A teacher, or
  • A representative from the applicant’s school.

Both letters should explain why the applicant deserves the scholarship and how they meet the scholarship selection criteria.

How to Apply

Step 1: Confirm Eligibility

Before beginning the application:

  • Review all scholarship requirements.
  • Confirm eligibility.
  • Choose an undergraduate program at RMIT Vietnam.

Step 2: Prepare Application Documents

Gather all required documents, including:

  • Academic records.
  • Personal statement.
  • Reference letters.
  • Identification documents.
  • Supporting evidence of financial or health circumstances.

Step 3: Upload Documents

  • Combine all supporting documents into a single PDF file.
  • Upload the file to an online storage platform such as Google Drive or OneDrive.

Step 4: Submit the Scholarship Application

  • Complete the 2026 RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship application form.
  • Include links to the uploaded supporting documents.
  • Submit the application before the deadline.

Important Dates

Scholarship Applications Open

12 January 2026

Scholarship Application Deadline

3 July 2026, 5:00 PM GMT+7

Scholarship Recipient Notification

25 September 2026

Important Notes for Applicants

  • Only shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews.
  • Interview schedules may be adjusted by the scholarship office.
  • Applicants whose transcripts do not include a yearly cumulative GPA will have their GPA calculated by the Scholarship Office.
  • Subjects graded on a pass/fail basis will not be included in GPA calculations.
  • The scholarship is highly competitive, and meeting eligibility requirements does not guarantee selection.
  • Applicants are encouraged to carefully review all scholarship terms and conditions before applying.

APPLY HERE

Why This Scholarship Matters

The RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship is more than financial assistance. It is an investment in talented young people who have demonstrated determination despite facing significant personal, social, financial, or health-related challenges.

By providing comprehensive support throughout an entire degree program, RMIT Vietnam is helping deserving students access quality higher education, develop professional skills, and create meaningful futures for themselves and their communities.

Students who meet the eligibility criteria are encouraged to prepare strong applications and submit them before the deadline of 3 July 2026.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post RMIT Vietnam Opportunity Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded Undergraduate Scholarship Covering 100% Tuition, Living Expenses, Accommodation Support, and More appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Hagley Museum and Library Grants and Fellowships 2026: Funding Opportunities for Researchers, Scholars, and Doctoral Candidates
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Researchers seeking financial support to access unique historical collections can benefit from the various grants and fellowships offered by the Hagley Museum and Library. Through the Center for the History of Business, Technology and Society, Hagley provides funding opportunities designed to support scholars conducting research in business history, technology, innovation, politics, and related fields. These […]

The post Hagley Museum and Library Grants and Fellowships 2026: Funding Opportunities for Researchers, Scholars, and Doctoral Candidates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers seeking financial support to access unique historical collections can benefit from the various grants and fellowships offered by the Hagley Museum and Library. Through the Center for the History of Business, Technology and Society, Hagley provides funding opportunities designed to support scholars conducting research in business history, technology, innovation, politics, and related fields.

These grants and fellowships help researchers offset the costs associated with extended research visits while also providing access to a vibrant scholarly community. Whether you are exploring Hagley’s collections for the first time or conducting intensive dissertation research, there are funding opportunities available to support your academic goals.

About the Hagley Museum and Library Grants and Fellowships

The Hagley Museum and Library is renowned for preserving and providing access to extensive historical collections that document the history of business, technology, industry, innovation, and society. To encourage the use of these valuable resources, Hagley offers several grants and fellowships that provide researchers with financial assistance during their stay.

Recipients gain more than just funding. They become part of a scholarly environment that encourages collaboration, discussion, and engagement with fellow researchers and experts.

Types of Grants Available

1. Exploratory Grants

Exploratory Grants are intended for researchers who want to determine whether Hagley’s collections are suitable for a larger research project.

Key features include:

  • Funding for a research visit of up to one week.
  • Opportunity to assess the relevance of Hagley’s collections.
  • Ideal for scholars considering a longer-term research project.
  • Can serve as a stepping stone toward a larger research grant application.

Researchers who initially receive an Exploratory Grant may later apply for a more extensive research grant if their preliminary visit demonstrates that the collections are highly relevant to their work.

2. Henry Belin du Pont Research Grants

The Henry Belin du Pont Research Grant is designed for researchers requiring more intensive use of Hagley’s collections.

Key features include:

  • Funding support for research visits lasting up to eight weeks.
  • Intended for substantial and in-depth research projects.
  • Supports scholars conducting extensive archival investigations.
  • Enables deeper engagement with Hagley’s historical resources.

This grant is particularly beneficial for academics whose projects require prolonged access to primary source materials.

Dissertation Fellowships for Advanced Graduate Students

Advanced graduate students pursuing dissertation research may apply for specialized fellowship opportunities.

1. Brandywine Dissertation Fellowship

The Brandywine Dissertation Fellowship provides support for doctoral candidates conducting dissertation research.

Benefits include:

  • Four-month fellowship period.
  • Dedicated time to conduct focused research.
  • Access to Hagley’s collections and scholarly community.
  • Opportunities for professional development and academic engagement.

2. Louis Galambos National Fellowship in Business and Politics

The Louis Galambos National Fellowship in Business and Politics supports advanced dissertation research in areas related to business and politics.

Benefits include:

  • Nine-month fellowship duration.
  • Extended support for comprehensive dissertation research.
  • Access to valuable archival materials.
  • Opportunities to interact with scholars working on related topics.

Benefits of Conducting Research at Hagley

Grant and fellowship recipients become active members of the Hagley Center’s intellectual community.

Participants may benefit from:

Scholarly Feedback Opportunities

Researchers can present works-in-progress during the Center’s brown bag lunch series, where Hagley staff and fellow scholars provide constructive feedback and discussion.

Podcast Participation

Recipients may share insights about their projects through the Hagley History Hangout podcast, helping to increase the visibility of their research.

Publication Opportunities

Researchers may contribute articles and research reflections to Hagley’s Collections and Research News platform.

Scholar Housing Community

Recipients have opportunities to interact with fellow scholars residing in Hagley’s on-site housing, fostering collaboration and networking.

Academic Events

Researchers are encouraged to participate in:

  • Author talks
  • Research seminars
  • Scholarly workshops
  • Special library events

These activities enrich the overall research experience and create opportunities for meaningful professional connections.

Application Requirements

All applicants must clearly address the following areas in their applications:

1. Contribution to Scholarship

Applicants should explain:

  • The significance of their research.
  • How their work contributes new knowledge to existing scholarship.
  • The academic impact of their proposed project.

2. Relevance of Hagley Collections

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • How Hagley’s collections support their research objectives.
  • Why access to Hagley materials is necessary for their project.
  • The specific resources they intend to use.

Important Application Advice

Prospective applicants are strongly encouraged to contact Hagley Library staff before submitting an application.

Doing so can help researchers:

  • Identify relevant collections.
  • Determine whether Hagley materials support their research.
  • Strengthen their applications.
  • Better prepare for their proposed visit.

Applicants are also encouraged to review previous researcher contributions in Collections and Research News and listen to episodes of the Hagley History Hangout podcast to learn how other scholars have successfully used Hagley’s resources.

Application Deadlines

Exploratory Grants and Henry Belin du Pont Research Grants

Applications are reviewed three times each year.

Deadlines:

  • March 31
  • June 30
  • October 31

Applicants can expect a decision within approximately 45 days after each deadline.

Fellowship Deadlines

Louis Galambos National Fellowship in Business and Politics

Deadline: February 1

Brandywine Dissertation Fellowship

Deadline: March 31

Applicants should review the specific fellowship requirements before submitting their applications.

Why These Opportunities Matter

The Hagley Museum and Library Grants and Fellowships provide researchers with more than financial assistance. They offer access to world-class historical collections, opportunities for scholarly engagement, professional networking, and a supportive research environment.

For scholars studying business history, technology, politics, innovation, industry, and related fields, these funding opportunities can significantly enhance the quality and impact of their research.

Researchers interested in utilizing Hagley’s collections are encouraged to prepare their applications early and ensure they clearly demonstrate the relevance and scholarly value of their proposed projects.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Hagley Museum and Library Grants and Fellowships 2026: Funding Opportunities for Researchers, Scholars, and Doctoral Candidates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AFS Global STEM Changemakers 2026: Fully Funded Virtual Exchange Program Offering 200 Scholarships for Young Sustainability Leaders Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Applications are now open for the AFS Global STEM Changemakers 2026 Program, a prestigious fully funded virtual exchange initiative designed to empower young people from around the world with the knowledge, skills, and global perspective needed to address today’s most pressing sustainability challenges. Organized by AFS Intercultural Programs and developed in collaboration with the University […]

The post AFS Global STEM Changemakers 2026: Fully Funded Virtual Exchange Program Offering 200 Scholarships for Young Sustainability Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the AFS Global STEM Changemakers 2026 Program, a prestigious fully funded virtual exchange initiative designed to empower young people from around the world with the knowledge, skills, and global perspective needed to address today’s most pressing sustainability challenges.

Organized by AFS Intercultural Programs and developed in collaboration with the University of Pennsylvania Center for Social Impact Strategy, this unique 12-week virtual learning experience provides young changemakers with an opportunity to strengthen their STEM knowledge, develop intercultural competencies, and create innovative solutions to real-world environmental and social challenges.

The program is offering 200 full scholarships to eligible youth globally, ensuring that talented students from diverse backgrounds can participate regardless of their financial circumstances.

About the AFS Global STEM Changemakers Program

The AFS Global STEM Changemakers Program is an immersive virtual exchange experience that combines STEM education, intercultural learning, social innovation, and sustainability leadership.

As global challenges such as climate change, environmental degradation, energy transition, and social inequality continue to affect communities worldwide, there is an increasing need for young leaders who can think critically, collaborate across cultures, and develop innovative solutions.

This program has been specifically designed to prepare the next generation of changemakers by providing practical learning experiences that bridge science, technology, sustainability, and social impact.

Participants will engage with students from different countries and cultural backgrounds while exploring some of the world’s most urgent sustainability issues.

What Participants Will Learn

Throughout the 12-week program, scholars will participate in an engaging and interactive curriculum focused on:

1. Sustainability and Global Challenges

Participants will gain a deeper understanding of:

  • Climate change
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Sustainable development
  • Energy transition
  • Global sustainability goals
  • Community resilience

2. STEM Education and Innovation

Students will strengthen their knowledge and skills in STEM-related fields through:

  • Interactive learning activities
  • Problem-solving exercises
  • Analytical thinking projects
  • Design thinking methodologies
  • Innovation-based learning approaches

3. Intercultural Competence

A major focus of the program is helping participants develop the ability to work effectively across cultures.

Students will learn:

  • Cross-cultural communication
  • Global citizenship
  • Intercultural understanding
  • Inclusive leadership
  • Collaboration in diverse teams
  • Bridge-building across communities

4. Social Innovation and Social Impact

Participants will explore:

  • Social entrepreneurship concepts
  • Community-centered problem solving
  • Human-centered design
  • Sustainable impact creation
  • Innovation for social good

Social Impact Capstone Project

One of the most exciting aspects of the program is the final capstone project.

During the program, participants will work on social impact presentations that propose innovative solutions to real-world sustainability challenges.

The capstone focuses particularly on:

  • Climate action
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Energy transition
  • Community development
  • Social innovation

This practical component allows students to apply what they learn while developing solutions that could positively impact communities around the world.

Scholarship Benefits

Selected scholars will receive a comprehensive package of benefits, including:

Full Scholarship Coverage

  • Complete participation in the AFS Global STEM Changemakers Program
  • Access to all virtual learning sessions
  • Participation in collaborative projects and workshops
  • Engagement with global peers and mentors

Advanced Certificate

Upon successful completion of the program, participants will earn:

Advanced Certificate on Global Competence for Social Impact

This certificate is jointly awarded by:

  • AFS Intercultural Programs
  • University of Pennsylvania Center for Social Impact Strategy

Global Alumni Network Access

Graduates will gain access to an exclusive AFS Global STEM alumni platform that offers:

  • Continued learning opportunities
  • Networking opportunities
  • Global connections
  • Professional development resources
  • Mentorship opportunities

Future Leadership Opportunities

Program alumni become eligible to participate in the AFS Youth Assembly, an international gathering that brings together young leaders working toward the achievement of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals.

Dedicated Scholarships for African Youth

As part of its commitment to expanding access and inclusion, AFS has allocated 100 scholarships specifically for young people from:

  • Angola
  • Ghana
  • Nigeria
  • Mauritania
  • Senegal

This targeted scholarship allocation aims to support youth leadership and innovation across Africa while ensuring greater representation in global sustainability discussions.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

AFS strongly encourages applications from students representing diverse backgrounds.

Importantly:

  • A minimum of 20% of scholarship places are reserved for indigenous and native community students.
  • Refugees are encouraged to apply.
  • Displaced students are encouraged to apply.
  • Students from underrepresented communities are highly encouraged to participate.

This commitment reflects the program’s goal of ensuring diverse voices contribute to solving global challenges.

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for the AFS Global STEM Changemakers 2026 Program, applicants must:

  • Be between 15 and 20 years old at the start of the program.
  • Have a birthdate between 1 December 2005 and 1 September 2011.
  • Demonstrate interest in STEM fields.
  • Show enthusiasm for sustainability and social impact.
  • Be willing to engage in intercultural learning experiences.
  • Participate actively throughout the virtual exchange program.

Program Timeline

Applicants should take note of the following important dates:

Application Opens

2 June 2026

Application Deadline

2 August 2026

Scholarship Decision Announcement

14 August 2026

Program Duration

31 August 2026 – 22 November 2026

Note: The timeline is subject to change.

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

The AFS Global STEM Changemakers Program is more than a virtual exchange initiative. It is a leadership development experience that combines STEM education, sustainability, intercultural competence, and social innovation.

Participants not only gain valuable academic and professional skills but also become part of a global network of young leaders committed to creating a more sustainable and equitable future.

For students passionate about science, technology, climate action, and social impact, this fully funded scholarship represents a unique opportunity to learn, collaborate, and make a difference on a global scale.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official AFS Global STEM Changemakers application portal before the deadline.

Application Deadline: 2 August 2026

 APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post AFS Global STEM Changemakers 2026: Fully Funded Virtual Exchange Program Offering 200 Scholarships for Young Sustainability Leaders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Carlson Family Foundation Youth Participatory Grantmaking Program 2025: Up to $20,000 in Funding for Youth-Focused Nonprofit Organizations
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The Carlson Family Foundation is inviting eligible nonprofit organizations to apply for its Youth Participatory Grantmaking Program, an innovative funding opportunity designed to empower local young people to influence community investment decisions. Through this unique initiative, youth leaders have played a central role in designing the grant application process, identifying community priorities, and reviewing applications […]

The post Carlson Family Foundation Youth Participatory Grantmaking Program 2025: Up to $20,000 in Funding for Youth-Focused Nonprofit Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Carlson Family Foundation is inviting eligible nonprofit organizations to apply for its Youth Participatory Grantmaking Program, an innovative funding opportunity designed to empower local young people to influence community investment decisions. Through this unique initiative, youth leaders have played a central role in designing the grant application process, identifying community priorities, and reviewing applications to recommend funding awards of up to $20,000.

This grant opportunity reflects the Foundation’s commitment to supporting organizations that advance equity and create meaningful opportunities for young people. Funding will be directed toward initiatives that address critical community needs in education, environmental protection, poverty reduction, and mental health.

Grant Overview

The Carlson Family Foundation Youth Participatory Grantmaking Program is a community-centered funding initiative where young people actively participate in the grantmaking process. Rather than relying solely on traditional review panels, local youth help determine which projects and organizations will receive support based on the priorities they believe matter most to their communities.

Selected organizations may receive grants of up to $20,000 to expand or strengthen programs that positively impact youth and young adults.

Key Dates

  • Application Deadline: End of Day (EOD), Friday, July 17
  • Funding Decisions Announced: Late August

Organizations interested in applying should ensure that all application materials and supporting documents are submitted before the deadline.

Funding Priorities

Applicants must demonstrate how their programs advance equity in at least one of the following focus areas:

1. K-12 Education

Organizations may focus on:

  • Literacy programs
  • Academic support services
  • Mentorship initiatives
  • College and career readiness
  • Workforce development programs
  • Educational enrichment opportunities

2. Environmental Protection

Eligible activities may include:

  • Protection of natural resources
  • Green space development and preservation
  • Environmental education
  • River and watershed protection
  • Sustainability projects
  • Community environmental engagement

3. Poverty Elimination

Programs may address:

  • Stable housing solutions
  • Food security initiatives
  • Economic empowerment
  • Workforce development
  • Financial literacy
  • Access to essential services

4. Mental Health

Organizations working in mental health may focus on:

  • Crisis intervention services
  • Wellness and prevention programs
  • Mental health screenings
  • Counseling support
  • Advocacy and awareness campaigns
  • Youth mental health resources

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for funding, organizations must meet all of the following criteria:

  • Have an annual operating budget of $1 million or less.
  • Hold an active 501(c)(3) nonprofit designation or operate under a fiscal sponsor.
  • Serve youth aged 24 years and below.
  • Operate within the Twin Cities 7-County Metro area.
  • Not be a current grantee of the Carlson Family Foundation.
  • Advance equity through programs aligned with one or more of the Foundation’s funding priorities.

Information Required in the Application

Applicants should be prepared to provide detailed organizational information during the application process.

Organization Details

Organizations will be asked to submit:

  • Legal organization name
  • Organization address
  • Organization website (if applicable)
  • Annual operating budget
  • Employer Identification Number (EIN), unless fiscally sponsored
  • Grant contact information:
    • Name
    • Job title
    • Email address
  • Organization leadership information (if different from the grant contact):
    • Name
    • Job title
    • Email address

Applicants must also indicate which primary focus area best aligns with their work:

  • K-12 Education
  • Environmental Protection
  • Poverty Elimination
  • Mental Health

Fiscal Sponsorship Information

Organizations operating under a fiscal sponsor must provide:

  • Memorandum of Understanding (MOU)
  • Fiscal sponsor contact details:
    • Name
    • Title
    • Email address

Demographic Information Requested

The Foundation seeks to better understand organizational and participant demographics through the application.

Applicants will be asked to provide:

Organizational Demographics

  • Percentage of staff who identify as Black, Indigenous, and People of Color (BIPOC)
  • Percentage of board members who identify as BIPOC

Participant Demographics

  • Number of participants served annually
  • Percentage identifying as BIPOC (optional)
  • Percentage identifying as LGBTQIA+ (optional)
  • Percentage identifying as having a physical, sensory, or cognitive disability (optional)

Age Groups Served

Applicants may select all applicable categories:

  • Grades K-5 (ages 5-11)
  • Middle School (grades 6-8, ages 11-14)
  • High School (grades 9-12, ages 14-18)
  • Young Adults (ages 18-24)
  • Adults (ages 24+)

Narrative Questions

Organizations will need to respond to four narrative questions, each with a maximum character limit of 2,000 characters.

Question 1

Tell us about your organization’s mission, programming, and what makes it unique.

Applicants should clearly explain their purpose, core activities, achievements, and distinguishing strengths.

Question 2

Describe the population(s) you serve and how you prioritize equity.

Organizations should discuss their target communities and explain how equity is integrated into program design and implementation.

Question 3

Describe how you elevate youth voice and leadership in your programming.

Since this is a youth-centered grant opportunity, applicants should provide concrete examples of how young people participate in leadership, decision-making, and program development.

Question 4

Describe the tools and resources your organization provides to young people that help them thrive.

Responses should highlight the services, supports, and opportunities that contribute to positive youth outcomes.

Required and Optional Supporting Documents

Required Attachment

  • Current Operating Budget

Optional Attachments

Organizations may strengthen their applications by including:

  • Photographs of programs and activities
  • Outreach materials
  • Flyers
  • Informational pamphlets
  • Community engagement resources

Why This Grant Matters

The Carlson Family Foundation Youth Participatory Grantmaking Program stands out because it places decision-making power in the hands of young people. By involving youth directly in the review and recommendation process, the Foundation ensures that funding reflects real community needs and priorities.

Organizations working to improve educational outcomes, protect the environment, reduce poverty, and support mental health can benefit from both financial support and recognition through this unique grant initiative.

Eligible organizations serving young people in the Twin Cities region are encouraged to submit a strong application before the July 17 deadline.

How to Apply

Interested organizations should prepare all required organizational information, narrative responses, and supporting documents before submitting their application.

For questions regarding the application process, organizations may contact:

Email: programs@carlsonfamilyfoundation.org

Applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their materials early to ensure a complete and competitive submission before the deadline.

CLICK HERE TO APPLY

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Grant Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Carlson Family Foundation Youth Participatory Grantmaking Program 2025: Up to $20,000 in Funding for Youth-Focused Nonprofit Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AI for Good Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Program for Social Impact Leaders Across Asia Pacific with Access to AVPN Global Conference in India
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping industries around the world, but for many social impact organizations, the challenge is no longer understanding what AI is—it is understanding how to implement it responsibly, strategically, and in alignment with organizational missions. To help bridge this gap, AVPN Academy, in partnership with DataDotOrg, has launched the inaugural AI for […]

The post AI for Good Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Program for Social Impact Leaders Across Asia Pacific with Access to AVPN Global Conference in India appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artificial intelligence is rapidly reshaping industries around the world, but for many social impact organizations, the challenge is no longer understanding what AI is—it is understanding how to implement it responsibly, strategically, and in alignment with organizational missions.

To help bridge this gap, AVPN Academy, in partnership with DataDotOrg, has launched the inaugural AI for Good Fellowship, a first-of-its-kind leadership program designed specifically for mid-to-senior social impact leaders across the Asia-Pacific region.

Unlike many AI training programs that focus primarily on tools and technical applications, the AI for Good Fellowship focuses on leadership, strategic decision-making, and organizational readiness. The program aims to empower leaders with the knowledge, frameworks, and practical skills necessary to responsibly adopt artificial intelligence and create sustainable impact within their organizations and communities.

For professionals working in philanthropy, nonprofits, social enterprises, development agencies, and impact investing organizations, this fellowship represents a unique opportunity to gain world-class AI knowledge while collaborating with fellow leaders facing similar challenges.

About the AI for Good Fellowship

The AI for Good Fellowship is an initiative developed through a collaboration between AVPN Academy and DataDotOrg. The fellowship seeks to equip social impact leaders with the ability to move beyond AI awareness and toward responsible implementation.

As AI technologies become increasingly influential, organizations are confronted with important questions such as:

  • How can AI be used ethically and responsibly?
  • How can organizations prepare for AI adoption?
  • Which AI solutions align with organizational missions?
  • What are the risks and opportunities associated with AI?
  • How can leaders build internal AI capacity while maintaining human-centered values?

The fellowship addresses these questions through a practical and collaborative learning experience.

What Makes the Fellowship Different?

Many AI training programs emphasize software tools and technical skills. However, the AI for Good Fellowship takes a broader perspective by focusing on:

1. Leadership and Strategic Decision-Making

Participants will learn how to:

  • Evaluate AI opportunities within their organizations.
  • Make responsible and informed decisions.
  • Align AI implementation with organizational goals.
  • Lead organizational transformation in an AI-driven world.

2. Organizational Readiness

The fellowship helps leaders understand:

  • AI governance frameworks.
  • Ethical considerations.
  • Risk management.
  • Capacity-building strategies.
  • Long-term sustainability of AI initiatives.

3. Practical Problem Solving

Participants will not only study concepts but will also:

  • Work on real organizational challenges.
  • Explore practical AI applications.
  • Collaborate with peers facing similar issues.
  • Exchange experiences and best practices.

Learn Alongside Leading Experts

The fellowship faculty includes experts from DataDotOrg, including:

Lindsey Gottschalk

Lindsey Gottschalk brings extensive experience in purpose-driven data and AI strategies, helping organizations harness emerging technologies to maximize social impact.

Priyank Hirani

Priyank Hirani specializes in AI capacity building and the development of responsible AI frameworks that empower organizations to innovate while maintaining ethical standards.

Together, they will guide fellows through the program and provide valuable insights into responsible AI adoption.

Benefits of the AI for Good Fellowship

Successful fellows will receive several benefits, including:

Leadership Development

Participants will strengthen their ability to lead AI transformation within mission-driven organizations.

Peer Learning

Fellows will engage with a network of like-minded professionals from across the Asia-Pacific region, creating opportunities for collaboration and long-term partnerships.

Real-World Application

Participants will work through actual organizational challenges and develop strategies that can immediately be implemented.

Access to AI Expertise

Fellows will learn directly from leading experts in data, AI capacity building, and social innovation.

Complimentary Access to the AVPN Global Conference 2026

One of the most exciting benefits of the fellowship is complimentary participation in the AVPN Global Conference 2026, which will be held in New Delhi, India.

The conference is one of the largest gatherings of social investors, philanthropists, foundations, and impact leaders in the world, providing fellows with exceptional networking and learning opportunities.

Global Exposure

Participants will become part of a growing ecosystem committed to using AI for social good and sustainable development.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is ideal for:

  • Nonprofit leaders.
  • Social entrepreneurs.
  • Foundation executives.
  • Impact investors.
  • Development practitioners.
  • Innovation managers.
  • Program directors.
  • Strategy and operations leaders.
  • Professionals responsible for organizational transformation and decision-making.

Applicants should have an interest in responsible AI implementation and a commitment to maximizing social impact.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Artificial intelligence has tremendous potential to address complex social challenges. However, successful AI adoption requires more than technology—it requires thoughtful leadership, ethical decision-making, and organizational preparedness.

The AI for Good Fellowship aims to cultivate a new generation of leaders who can responsibly leverage AI to strengthen organizations and improve lives across the Asia-Pacific region.

As AI continues to shape the future, this fellowship provides a timely opportunity for impact leaders to develop the skills, networks, and strategic insights needed to ensure that technology serves humanity and creates meaningful change.

How to Apply

Interested candidates can submit their applications through the official application portal:

Deadline

Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible. No specific application deadline has been announced at the time of publication.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here

The post AI for Good Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Program for Social Impact Leaders Across Asia Pacific with Access to AVPN Global Conference in India appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: DRK + NLV Navigation Technology Initiative: Up to $800,000 in Funding and Support for AI and Tech Startups Expanding Economic Opportunity in America
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Draper Richards Kaplan Foundation (DRK), in collaboration with NextLadder Ventures (NLV), has launched an ambitious initiative aimed at supporting early-stage organizations using technology and artificial intelligence to improve economic mobility among low-income Americans. Known as the DRK + NLV Navigation Technology Initiative, the program seeks to back innovative startups and nonprofit organizations that are […]

The post DRK + NLV Navigation Technology Initiative: Up to $800,000 in Funding and Support for AI and Tech Startups Expanding Economic Opportunity in America appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Draper Richards Kaplan Foundation (DRK), in collaboration with NextLadder Ventures (NLV), has launched an ambitious initiative aimed at supporting early-stage organizations using technology and artificial intelligence to improve economic mobility among low-income Americans.

Known as the DRK + NLV Navigation Technology Initiative, the program seeks to back innovative startups and nonprofit organizations that are building practical tools to help individuals and families navigate complex systems such as healthcare, housing, workforce development, public benefits, and financial services.

Millions of Americans continue to face barriers in accessing critical resources and opportunities due to fragmented systems, outdated processes, and limited support infrastructure. Through this initiative, DRK and NLV aim to empower organizations creating technology-enabled solutions that make these systems easier to access and more effective.

Why the Initiative Matters

Across the United States, millions of people qualify for public benefits they never receive. Others remain on housing waiting lists for years, struggle to access healthcare, or find it difficult to secure better jobs and financial stability.

Frontline workers such as:

  • Community health workers
  • Case managers
  • Career counselors
  • Housing advocates
  • Legal aid attorneys

often face overwhelming workloads and limited technological support.

The DRK + NLV Navigation Technology Initiative seeks to bridge these gaps through scalable technology and AI-powered solutions that improve access and create pathways toward economic dignity and mobility.

Funding and Benefits

Selected organizations will receive substantial support, including:

1. Direct Funding

Successful applicants will receive:

  • $300,000 in catalytic funding.

2. In-Kind Operational Support

Organizations may receive:

  • Up to $500,000 worth of operational support.

This support can help founders strengthen their organizations and accelerate growth.

3. Access to Expert Networks

Participants gain access to:

  • Experienced operators.
  • Investors.
  • Social impact leaders.
  • Entrepreneurs and mentors.
  • Strategic partnerships and collaborations.

Together, these resources provide a strong foundation for organizations seeking to scale their impact.

Priority Areas

DRK and NextLadder Ventures are particularly interested in organizations developing solutions in the following sectors:

Housing Access

Projects that:

  • Help renters secure and maintain housing.
  • Support homeless populations.
  • Improve housing counseling services.
  • Increase access to affordable housing opportunities.

Examples include:

  • Eviction prevention platforms.
  • Housing navigation tools.
  • Software for case managers.

Benefits Access

Solutions that:

  • Simplify public benefit enrollment.
  • Improve benefit retention.
  • Reduce administrative bottlenecks.
  • Help individuals utilize available support programs.

Examples include:

  • AI-assisted benefits enrollment.
  • Medicaid renewal tools.
  • Public assistance navigation platforms.

Healthcare Access

Organizations creating solutions that:

  • Connect underserved communities to healthcare.
  • Improve healthcare coverage.
  • Assist community health workers.
  • Expand healthcare navigation systems.

Examples include:

  • Medical financial navigation tools.
  • Care coordination platforms.
  • Software for healthcare providers serving vulnerable populations.

Workforce Mobility

Technologies that help people:

  • Access skills training.
  • Obtain credentials.
  • Find higher-paying jobs.
  • Improve career progression.

Examples include:

  • Workforce navigation platforms.
  • Career development tools.
  • Employment support technologies.

Financial Stability

Projects aimed at:

  • Helping individuals manage financial shocks.
  • Accessing tax credits and income support.
  • Reducing debt burdens.
  • Managing benefit cliffs.
  • Improving financial decision-making.

Who Should Apply?

The initiative welcomes both:

  • For-profit companies.
  • Nonprofit organizations (501(c)(3)).

Applicants should possess:

A Working Product

Organizations should have:

  • A functional product or service.
  • Demonstrated customer demand.
  • At least one paying or contracted customer.

Financial Sustainability

Most selected organizations will have:

  • Annualized revenue or committed funding between $600,000 and $3 million.
  • A clear pathway to sustainable growth.
  • Potential to serve hundreds of thousands or millions of people over the next decade.

Founder Fit

DRK prioritizes founders who have:

  • Lived experience with the challenges faced by their target communities.
  • Frontline experience.
  • Strong relationships with beneficiaries and users.

Impact Potential

Organizations should demonstrate:

  • Evidence of positive outcomes.
  • Strong social impact.
  • Ability to scale solutions nationally.

Geographic Eligibility

The initiative focuses on:

  • US-based organizations.
  • Organizations serving populations across the United States.
  • Communities with the greatest access gaps.

Rather than prioritizing locations with better infrastructure, the initiative places emphasis on areas where the need for support is greatest.

Rolling Applications: No Fixed Deadline

One of the major advantages of the DRK + NLV Navigation Technology Initiative is that applications are accepted on a rolling basis.

This means founders and nonprofit leaders can apply at any time without worrying about a strict submission deadline. However, early applications are encouraged, as funding opportunities are competitive.

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

Unlike traditional grants that provide only funding, this initiative combines capital, strategic support, mentorship, and access to powerful networks.

Organizations selected through the DRK + NLV Navigation Technology Initiative gain an opportunity to accelerate growth while creating lasting social impact for underserved populations.

For entrepreneurs and nonprofit leaders building AI-powered solutions that increase economic opportunity, financial stability, and human dignity, this initiative represents one of the most compelling social innovation funding opportunities available today.

How to Apply

Interested founders and nonprofit leaders can submit applications through the official website

Click here to learn more about this opportunity

Deadline

Applications are accepted on a rolling basis. There is currently no fixed deadline.

Discover more global grants opportunities on OFY here

The post DRK + NLV Navigation Technology Initiative: Up to $800,000 in Funding and Support for AI and Tech Startups Expanding Economic Opportunity in America appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Utah Office of AI Policy Hiring Policy Advisor and Senior Policy Researcher to Shape the Future of AI Governance
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence is transforming economies, industries, governments, and societies around the world. As policymakers work to balance innovation with responsible governance, opportunities are emerging for professionals who want to help shape the future of AI regulation and policy development. The Utah Office of AI Policy (OAIP) has announced recruitment for two […]

The post Utah Office of AI Policy Hiring Policy Advisor and Senior Policy Researcher to Shape the Future of AI Governance appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence is transforming economies, industries, governments, and societies around the world. As policymakers work to balance innovation with responsible governance, opportunities are emerging for professionals who want to help shape the future of AI regulation and policy development.

The Utah Office of AI Policy (OAIP) has announced recruitment for two key positions: Policy Advisor and Senior Policy Researcher. These roles offer a rare opportunity to work directly on real-world AI governance challenges while contributing to one of the most innovative state-level AI policy initiatives in the United States.

According to Zachary Boyd, Director of Utah’s Office of AI Policy and Assistant Professor at Brigham Young University, these positions are designed for individuals who are passionate about solving practical AI policy problems and helping governments navigate the opportunities and risks created by artificial intelligence.

About the Utah Office of AI Policy

The Utah Office of AI Policy has gained national attention for its proactive approach to artificial intelligence governance. Rather than focusing solely on theoretical discussions about AI regulation, the office actively develops practical policy solutions that can be implemented across sectors.

The office works closely with:

  • State legislators
  • Government agencies
  • Business leaders
  • Technology innovators
  • Academic researchers
  • Community stakeholders
  • Ordinary citizens affected by AI technologies

Its mission is to help governments encourage innovation while responsibly addressing emerging risks associated with artificial intelligence.

Available Positions

1. Policy Advisor

The Policy Advisor will play a central role in helping the office address AI-related policy issues across multiple sectors and industries.

Key areas of work may include:

  • AI governance frameworks
  • Legislative analysis
  • Regulatory development
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Public policy strategy
  • Risk assessment
  • Innovation policy
  • Cross-sector collaboration

This role is ideal for professionals who enjoy translating complex technical developments into practical policy solutions.

2. Senior Policy Researcher

The Senior Policy Researcher position focuses on generating evidence-based insights that support AI policymaking and governance initiatives.

Responsibilities may include:

  • Conducting policy research
  • Evaluating AI governance models
  • Producing policy reports
  • Analyzing emerging AI developments
  • Supporting legislative initiatives
  • Developing policy recommendations
  • Engaging with experts and stakeholders
